codeReview.summary: The feature satisfies the core VAL-VISUAL-016 contract: the visible ASCII layer is now canvas-based, Hermes-sourced, multi-density, and visibly animated instead of relying on the rejected DOM <pre> grid. I found two non-blocking correctness gaps around the claimed fallback path and the claimed feedback-buffer decay behavior.

issues.description: The advertised non-canvas fallback never activates. If `canvas.getContext("2d")` fails here, the renderer returns without drawing or signaling any fallback, while `HeroVisualSystem` still mounts `AsciiBackground` (`src/components/visual/HeroVisualSystem.tsx:82`) and the stylesheet keeps that DOM fallback permanently hidden with `opacity: 0` (`src/app/globals.css:179`). In a real 2D-canvas failure path, users would get no ASCII atmosphere at all despite the code/comments claiming a retained non-canvas fallback.

The feedback buffer does not preserve decayed history the way the comments and handoff claim. `this.bufCtx.drawImage(this.buffer, 0, 0)` is immediately followed by `clearRect(...)` and a full redraw of the current frame, so prior-frame content is discarded before it can accumulate into multi-frame trails. The renderer still has visible motion from its evolving value fields, but the advertised Hermes-style decayed feedback loop is not actually implemented.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared guidance requires `agent-browser`, but it does not capture the current CLI invocation shape. This worker first tried the unsupported `launch` subcommand, then had to inspect `agent-browser --help` before switching to `open`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows a failed browser step with `Unknown command: launch`, immediately followed by `agent-browser --help` and then a successful `agent-browser ... open http://127.0.0.1:3100` flow.

summary: Pass. The shipped feature replaces the visible DOM ASCII grid with a real-time Hermes-derived canvas renderer and aligns with the main VAL-VISUAL-016 goal. Non-blocking follow-ups: wire the claimed non-canvas fallback so it can actually surface when 2D canvas init fails, and fix the feedback-buffer update path so decay produces real multi-frame trails instead of being cleared away immediately.
